safetyTips.description: The most important action during an earthquake

safetyTips.title: Stay Away from Windows
safetyTips.description: Glass can shatter and cause serious injuries

safetyTips.description: Identify safe places in every room of your home

safetyTips.title: Check for Injuries First
safetyTips.description: Provide first aid before checking property damage

safetyTips.title: Turn Off Gas if You Smell Leak
safetyTips.description: Prevent fires and explosions after earthquake

reminders.title: Monthly Drill Practice
reminders.description: Practice earthquake drills with your family every month

reminders.title: Check Emergency Kit
reminders.description: Inspect and rotate supplies every 6 months

reminders.title: Update Emergency Contacts
reminders.description: Keep your family contact list current

reminders.title: Review Evacuation Routes
reminders.description: Familiarize yourself with updated evacuation paths

reminders.title: Secure Water Heater

drillTutorials.title: Duck, Cover, and Hold
drillTutorials.description: Learn the essential earthquake response technique that can save your life

steps.title: DROP
steps.description: Drop down onto your hands and knees immediately. This position protects you from being knocked down and allows you to stay low and crawl to shelter if nearby.
steps.tip: Do this before the earthquake knocks you down

steps.title: COVER
steps.description: Cover your head and neck with your arms. If a sturdy table or desk is nearby, crawl underneath it for shelter. If no shelter is nearby, crawl next to an interior wall away from windows.
steps.tip: Stay on your knees and bend over to protect vital organs

steps.title: HOLD ON
steps.description: If you are under a table or desk, hold on to it with one hand and be ready to move with it if it shifts. If seated and unable to drop to the floor, bend forward, cover your head with your arms and hold on to your neck with both hands.
steps.tip: Stay in position until the shaking stops

steps.title: STAY PUT
steps.description: Remain in your safe position for at least 60 seconds after the shaking stops. Do not immediately run outside as aftershocks may occur.
steps.tip: Count to 60 slowly before moving

steps.title: ASSESS SITUATION
steps.description: After shaking stops, carefully look around. Check for hazards such as fire, gas leaks, or structural damage before moving.
steps.tip: Use your phone flashlight if power is out

importantNotes: DO NOT run outside during shaking
DO NOT stand in doorways (outdated advice)
If in bed, stay there and cover your head with a pillow
If outdoors, move away from buildings, trees, and power lines
If driving, pull over safely and stay in vehicle

drillTutorials.title: Evacuation Drill
drillTutorials.description: Master the proper evacuation procedures for your home and workplace

steps.title: IDENTIFY EXITS
steps.description: Know all possible exits from your location. Identify at least two escape routes from every room. Mark them on a floor plan.
steps.tip: Walk through each route during daylight

steps.title: GRAB GO-BAG
steps.description: Keep a pre-packed emergency bag near the exit. It should contain essentials: water, first aid, flashlight, phone charger, important documents.
steps.tip: Store go-bag in an easily accessible location

steps.title: ALERT OTHERS
steps.description: Shout 'EVACUATE!' loudly and clearly. Knock on doors if in a building. Help children, elderly, and people with disabilities first.
steps.tip: Assign roles to family members in advance

steps.title: MOVE QUICKLY BUT SAFELY
steps.description: Walk quickly but do not run. Stay low if there is smoke. Use stairs, never elevators. Keep to the right side of hallways and stairs.
steps.tip: Feel doors before opening - if hot, use alternate route

steps.title: GO TO ASSEMBLY POINT
steps.description: Proceed directly to your designated meeting point. This should be at least 50 meters from the building in an open area away from power lines and trees.
steps.tip: Choose a landmark everyone can easily identify

steps.title: ACCOUNT FOR EVERYONE
steps.description: Take attendance at the assembly point. Report missing persons to emergency responders immediately. Do not re-enter the building.
steps.tip: Designate one person as the accountability officer

steps.title: WAIT FOR ALL-CLEAR
steps.description: Stay at the assembly point until authorities declare it safe to return. Listen to official announcements via radio or E-TIPS app.
steps.tip: Keep phone charged for emergency communications

importantNotes: Practice evacuation drills at least twice a year
Time your evacuation to identify bottlenecks
Ensure everyone knows the assembly point location
Keep evacuation routes clear of obstacles
Have a backup meeting point in case primary is blocked
Never use elevators during evacuation

drillTutorials.title: Fire Safety Drill
drillTutorials.description: Learn how to respond to fires that may occur after earthquakes

steps.title: DETECT FIRE
steps.description: Recognize signs of fire: smoke, smell of burning, heat, or visible flames. Shout 'FIRE!' to alert others immediately.
steps.tip: Install smoke detectors on every floor

steps.title: ACTIVATE ALARM
steps.description: Pull the nearest fire alarm if in a building. Call emergency services (911 or local fire department) immediately.
steps.tip: Know where fire alarms are located

steps.title: EVACUATE IMMEDIATELY
steps.description: Leave the area at once. Close doors behind you to slow fire spread. Do not stop to collect belongings.
steps.tip: Touch doors before opening - if hot, use another exit

steps.title: STAY LOW
steps.description: Crawl low under smoke. Hot air and toxic gases rise, so cleaner air is near the floor. Cover your nose and mouth with cloth.
steps.tip: Practice crawling with eyes closed

steps.title: NEVER GO BACK
steps.description: Once outside, stay outside. Never return to a burning building for any reason. Wait for firefighters.
steps.tip: Account for everyone at assembly point

importantNotes: Stop, Drop, and Roll if clothes catch fire
Know how to use a fire extinguisher (PASS method)
Keep fire extinguisher accessible
Check smoke detectors monthly
Have escape ladders for upper floors

drillTutorials.title: First Aid Response
drillTutorials.description: Essential first aid skills for earthquake emergencies

steps.title: ASSESS THE SCENE
steps.description: Ensure the area is safe before approaching victims. Look for hazards: fire, gas leaks, unstable structures, downed power lines.
steps.tip: Your safety comes first - you can't help if you're injured

steps.title: CHECK RESPONSIVENESS
steps.description: Tap the person's shoulder and shout 'Are you okay?' If no response, check for breathing by looking at chest movement.
steps.tip: Call for help immediately if person is unresponsive

steps.title: CONTROL BLEEDING
steps.description: Apply direct pressure to wounds with clean cloth. Elevate injured limb above heart if possible. Apply pressure for 10-15 minutes.
steps.tip: Use gloves or plastic bag to protect yourself

steps.title: TREAT FOR SHOCK
steps.description: Lay person flat, elevate legs 12 inches if no spinal injury suspected. Keep warm with blanket. Reassure and monitor.
steps.tip: Do not give food or water to shock victims

steps.title: IMMOBILIZE INJURIES
steps.description: Do not move person with suspected spinal injury. Splint broken bones before moving. Use rigid materials and padding.
steps.tip: Splint in position found, don't try to straighten

steps.title: MONITOR VITAL SIGNS
steps.description: Check breathing, pulse, and consciousness every 5 minutes. Note any changes. Keep detailed records for medical responders.
steps.tip: Use your phone to time and record observations

importantNotes: Take a certified first aid course
Keep first aid kit fully stocked
Know CPR and AED use
Do not move victims unless in immediate danger
Document all care provided

items.name: Water
items.quantity: 1 gallon per person per day for 3 days
items.notes: Store in sealed containers, rotate every 6 months

items.name: Non-perishable Food
items.notes: Canned goods, energy bars, dried fruits, crackers

items.name: Manual Can Opener
items.notes: Essential if you have canned food

items.name: First Aid Kit
items.notes: Bandages, gauze, tape, scissors, tweezers, antiseptic

items.name: Prescription Medications
items.notes: Keep list of medications and dosages

items.name: Medical Supplies
items.notes: Thermometer, blood pressure monitor, glucose meter

items.name: Battery-powered Radio
items.notes: NOAA Weather Radio if possible, with extra batteries

items.name: Moist Towelettes
items.notes: For personal hygiene when water is limited

items.name: Change of Clothes
items.notes: Long pants, long-sleeve shirt, sturdy shoes

items.name: Important Documents
items.notes: IDs, insurance policies, bank records, medical records

items.name: Cash
items.notes: Small bills and coins, ATMs may not work

items.name: Emergency Contact List
items.notes: Phone numbers of family, doctors, insurance
